page.title: dermatomyositis — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for dermatomyositis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for dermatomyositis: 194 alterations and 162 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Creatine supplements combined with home exercises improve functional performance without significant adverse effects in patients with polymyositis or dermatomyositis.
Dermatomyositis may be associated with celiac disease even in the absence of gastrointestinal symptoms.
Lupus erythematosus and other autoimmune diseases related to statin therapy have been reported.
Tobacco smoking was associated with clinical and autoantibody phenotypes in Caucasians.
A dermatomyositis-like syndrome has been linked to statin drug use.
Hepatitis B vaccine associated with dermatomyositis has been reported.
Remission of recalcitrant dermatomyositis following a chiropractic adjustment.
This describes four cases of auto-immune reactions after tetanus vaccination.
Hepatitis B vaccines have a potential to occasionally trigger the onset of immune diseases in individuals with an underlying genetic or immunological susceptibility.
Statin drugs are known in some cases to induce autoimmune myopathies such as dermatomyositis, polymyositis, and immune-mediated necrotizing myopathies (IMNM).

alterations.name: Diffuse reticular or finely nodular infiltrations
Respiratory insufficiency due to muscle weakness
